content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of NNN powers of an integer kkk; let's denote the iii-th of these powers by kAik^{A_i}kAi​. You should partition this sequence into two non-empty **contiguous** subsequences; each element of the original sequence should appear in exactly one of these subsequences. In addition, the product of (the sum of elements of the left subsequence) and (the sum of elements of the right subsequence) should be maximum possible.

Find the smallest position at which you should split this sequence in such a way that this product is maximized.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and k. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the size of the left subsequence. If there is more than one possible answer, print the smallest possible one.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The actual sequence of powers is [2^1, 2^1, 2^3, 2^3, 2^5] = [2, 2, 8, 8, 32]. The maximum product is 20 * 32 = 640. In the optimal solution, the sequence is partitioned into [2, 2, 8, 8] and [32].
The actual sequence of powers is [3^1, 3^2, 3^3] = [3, 9, 27]. The maximum product is 12 * 27 = 324. In the optimal solution, the sequence is partitioned into [3, 9] and [27].
The actual sequence of powers is [2^0, 2^1, 2^2, 2^3] = [1, 2, 4, 8]. The maximum product is 7 * 8 = 56. In the optimal solution, the sequence is partitioned into [1, 2, 4] and [8].

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over all possible partition points in the sequence and calculate the product of the sum of the left subsequence and the sum of the right subsequence. We keep track of the maximum product and the corresponding partition point. The time complexity of this approach is O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the sequence.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the sequence
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the sequence and the prefix sums

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, k = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    maxProduct = -1
    partitionPoint = -1
    for i in range(1, N):
        leftSum = sum(k * A[j] for j in range(i))
        rightSum = sum(k * A[j] for j in range(i, N))
        product = leftSum * rightSum
        if product > maxProduct:
            maxProduct = product
            partitionPoint = i
    print(partitionPoint)
